body.title: Break the proxy<->domain circular dependency: device builds RouteRequest, delete BaseDomain.select()
body.description:
device.py (the inference PROXY) imports domains.resolve_domain and calls domain.select() in 3 places; domains/base.py imports agentic_loop; agentic_loop does a function-local lazy `from ...device import InferenceDevice` and constructs one. That is a CYCLE (device -> domains -> agentic_loop -> device), hidden by the lazy import. Root cause: BaseDomain glues two opposite-facing responsibilities — select() is consumed BY the proxy, run() consumes the proxy.

select() is a pure pass-through: it builds RouteRequest(domain=self.name, ticket_tier=bridge(task_class), ...) and calls rules_engine.resolve(). device.py already has request.domain and request.task_class, so it can build the RouteRequest itself.

WORK: move _TASK_CLASS_TO_TIER into dimensions.py (the routing layer that owns the vocabulary); have device.dispatch build RouteRequest and call self._rules.resolve() directly; DELETE BaseDomain.select() and the domains import from device.py. After this, unseen_university/devices/inference/ (the routing core) imports NOTHING from domains/ — which makes the later relocation of domains a pure directory move.

**Affected files:** device.py, domains/base.py, dimensions.py

**Scope boundary:** no behavior change to selection; the agentic loop is NOT moved here (T-inference-move-agentic-loop-to-domain).

**Completion criteria:** device.py has no `domains` import; BaseDomain has no select(); resolve picks are unchanged.

**Test plan:** red->green — an import-graph assertion that no module in the routing core (device/rules_engine/sources/models_registry/connections/policy/dimensions) imports domains or agentic_loop, plus selection-parity on the live coding path (coding -> devstral@ollama, tool-capable).

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: CLOSED PROVEN (proof cc.0.the-inference-proxy-is-a-leaf-the-routin @343c20f3, red=AssertionError -> green=passed). device.py imported domains.resolve_domain to call BaseDomain.select() (3 sites); domains/base.py imports agentic_loop; agentic_loop reached back with a function-local 'from ...device import InferenceDevice' and constructed one => cycle device -> domains -> agentic_loop -> device, hidden by the lazy import. Root cause: BaseDomain glued two opposite-facing responsibilities (select() consumed BY the proxy, run() CONSUMES the proxy), and select() was a pure pass-through building a RouteRequest from the domain's own name that the proxy already had as request.domain. FIX: dimensions.py owns TASK_CLASS_TO_TIER + route_request() (the single live-dispatch construction point); device.py builds its own RouteRequest via InferenceDevice._route() and calls resolve() directly; BaseDomain.select() DELETED. Layering is now one-directional: worker -> domain (loop+escalation+prompts) -> proxy (routing+dispatch). Pinned structurally by test_proxy_domain_layering.py, which reads the import graph with ast.walk so a function-local import cannot re-hide the cycle. Suites: 540 passed (inference+scraps+granny); 3 pre-existing reds, ticketed.
